catalog.title: The Hobbit
catalog.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
catalog.description: Bilbo Baggins embarks on an unexpected journey with dwarves to reclaim treasure from a dragon.

catalog.author: George Orwell
catalog.description: Winston Smith lives in a dystopian world controlled by Big Brother where thought is policed.

catalog.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
catalog.author: Harper Lee
catalog.description: Scout Finch learns about prejudice and justice in the Deep South during the 1930s.

catalog.title: Pride and Prejudice
catalog.author: Jane Austen
catalog.description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates love, marriage, and social class in Regency England.

catalog.title: The Great Gatsby
catalog.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
catalog.description: Jay Gatsby's obsessive pursuit of lost love amid 1920s excess and corruption.

catalog.title: Sapiens
catalog.author: Yuval Noah Harari
catalog.description: History of humankind from the Stone Age to the 21st century and our cognitive revolution.

catalog.title: Dune
catalog.author: Frank Herbert
catalog.description: Paul Atreides battles for control of the desert planet Arrakis and its valuable spice.

catalog.title: Clean Code
catalog.author: Robert C. Martin
catalog.description: Practical guide to writing maintainable, readable code with real-world examples for developers.

catalog.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
catalog.author: J.K. Rowling
catalog.description: Young wizard Harry discovers his magical heritage and battles dark forces at Hogwarts.

catalog.title: The Da Vinci Code
catalog.author: Dan Brown
catalog.description: Robert Langdon uncovers religious conspiracies while solving cryptic puzzles in Paris.

catalog.title: Atomic Habits
catalog.author: James Clear
catalog.description: Small changes for remarkable results through better habit formation and breaking bad ones.

catalog.title: The Alchemist
catalog.author: Paulo Coelho
catalog.description: Santiago the shepherd follows his Personal Legend across deserts to find treasure.

catalog.title: Brave New World
catalog.author: Aldous Huxley
catalog.description: A dystopian society where citizens are genetically engineered and pacified by pleasure.
